X-Next-Cursor.description: The cursor value to use for the next page of results

X-Request-Id.description: A request ID provided by the client. If not provided, the server will generate one. Will be reflected in responses.

X-Smallstep-Api-Version.description: API version to use. Will be reflected in responses.

accept.description: The content type the client is willing to accept. Also includes API version.
accept.name: Accept

authorityDomainOrID.description: ID or domain of the authority
authorityDomainOrID.name: authorityDomainOrID

browserID.description: The UUID of the browser mTLS configuation.
browserID.name: browserID

deviceFilters.description: Filter a list of devices by specifying the critera that must be met. Example: `?filters[os]=windows&filters[assurance]=high`, which after encoding would be `?filters%5Bos%5D=windows&filters%5Bassurance%5D=high`
deviceFilters.name: filters

assurance.description: Assurance levels that devices must match.

onlyManaged.description: Only return devices that are Smallstep-managed.

onlyPendingApproval.description: Only return devices that are pending approval.

onlyUnassigned.description: Only return devices that do not have a user binding.

operatingSystem.description: Operating systems that devices must match.

ownership.description: Ownership values that devices must match.

source.description: Registration sources that devices must match.

deviceID.description: Either the UUID of the device, or a URN that uniquely identifies the device. URNs must be URL-encoded, but provided examples are shown without encoding for clarity.

ekPub.summary: The SHA-256 hash of the device's TPM Endorsement Key, in URN form.

permanentId.summary: A URN referencing the permanent identifier of the device.

sn.summary: A URN referencing the serial number of the device.

uuid.summary: The UUID of the device in the Smallstep inventory.

deviceID.name: deviceID

deviceSort.description: The sorting behavior for matching devices. Example: `?sort[field]=lastSeen&sort[ascending]=true`, which after encoding would be `?sort%5Bfield%5D=lastSeen&sort%5Bascending%5D=true`
deviceSort.name: sort

ascending.description: Indicates that devices should be sorted in ascending order.

field.description: The field used for sorting matching devices.

ethernetID.description: The UUID of the Ethernet device configuration.
ethernetID.name: ethernetID

idpClientID.description: The UUID of the IdP client.
idpClientID.name: idpClientID

managedRadiusID.description: The UUID of the managed RADIUS server.
managedRadiusID.name: managedRadiusID

pagination.description: Paginate over a list of objects. Example: `?pagination[first]=30&pagination[after]=MTIzNA==`, which after encoding would be `?pagination%5Bfirst%5D=30&pagination%5Bafter%5D=MTIzNA==`
pagination.name: pagination

after.description:
Fetch a page of results other than the first page.
Must be copied from a previously returned X-Next-Cursor header.

first.description:
Limits the number of results returned.
Defaults to 100.

provisionerNameOrID.description: Either the name or UUID of the provisioner
provisionerNameOrID.name: provisionerNameOrID

requestID.description: A request ID provided by the client. If not provided, the server will generate one. Will be reflected in responses.
requestID.name: X-Request-Id

secret.description: Whether to return the managed RADIUS secret in the response.
secret.name: secret

vpnID.description: The UUID of the VPN device configuration.
vpnID.name: vpnID

webhookNameOrID.description: Either the name or UUID of the provisioner webhook
webhookNameOrID.name: webhookNameOrID

wifiID.description: The UUID of the Wi-Fi device configuration.
wifiID.name: wifiID

id.description: A UUID identifying this account. Read only.

account.description: An account configures the certificate a device uses to connect to a protected resource.

accountRequest.description: The configuration settings of an account.

name.description: A human-friendly name for this account.

type.description: The type of configuration settings for this account.

acmeAttestationProvisioner.description: A [provisioner](https://smallstep.com/docs/step-ca/provisioners/#acme) that enables automation with the [device-attest-01 challenge of the ACME protocol](https://smallstep.com/blog/acme-managed-device-attestation-explained/).

attestationFormats.description: The allowed attestation formats for the device-attest-01 challenge. Valid values are `apple`, `step`, and `tpm`. The apple format is for Apple devices, and adds trust for Apple's CAs. The step format is for non-TPM devices that can issue attestation certificates, such as YubiKey PIV. It adds trust for Yubico's root CA. The tpm format is for TPMs and does not trust any CAs by default.

attestationRoots.description: A trust bundle of root certificates in PEM format that will be used to verify attestation certificates. The default value depends on the value of attestationFormats. If provided, this PEM bundle will override the CA trust established by setting attestationFormats to apple or step. At least one root certificate is required when using the tpm attestationFormat.

forceCN.description: Force one of the SANs to become the Common Name, if a Common Name is not provided.

requireEAB.description: Only ACME clients that have been preconfigured with valid EAB credentials will be able to create an account with this provisioner.

acmeAttestationProvisioner.title: ACME Device Attestation Provisioner

acmeProvisioner.description: A [provisioner](https://smallstep.com/docs/step-ca/provisioners/#acme) that enables automation with the [ACME protocol](https://smallstep.com/docs/step-ca/acme-basics/#acme-challenges).

challenges.description: Which ACME challenge types are allowed.

forceCN.description: Force one of the SANs to become the Common Name, if a Common Name is not provided.

requireEAB.description: Only ACME clients that have been preconfigured with valid EAB credentials will be able to create an account with this provisioner. Must be `true` for all new provisioners.

acmeProvisioner.title: ACME Provisioner

authority.description: An X509 authority hosted by Smallstep.

activeRevocation.description: Whether CRL and OCSP are enabled (advanced authorities only).

adminEmails.description: Users that have admin access to manage the authority.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the authority was created.

domain.description: The domain where the authority can be reached.

fingerprint.description: The SHA-256 digest of the authority's root certificate in hex format.

root.description: The root certificate in pem format.

authority.title: Authority

authorityCsr.description: A certificate signing request for an X509 advanced authority with an external root.

csr.description: A certificate sigining request for the authority's intermediate issuer in pem format.

authorityCsr.title: Authority CSR

authorityType.description: One of the available authority types
authorityType.title: Authority Type

awsAccounts.description: The list of AWS account IDs that are allowed to use an AWS cloud provisioner.
awsAccounts.title: AWS Accounts

awsDisableCustomSANs.description: By default custom SANs are valid, but if this option is set to `true` only the SANs available in the instance identity document will be valid. These are the private IP and the DNS ip-<private-ip>.<region>.compute.internal.

name.description: A friendly name for this AWS account.

disableTrustOnFirstUse.description: By default only one certificate will be granted per instance, but if the option is set to `true` this limit is not set and different tokens can be used to get different certificates.

azureAudience.description: Defaults to https://management.azure.com/ but it can be changed if necessary.
azureAudience.title: Azure Audience

resourceGroups.description: A list of resource groups that are allowed to enroll with the Smallstep Platform.

disableTrustOnFirstUse.description: By default only one certificate will be granted per instance, but if the option is set to true this limit is not set and different tokens can be used to get different certificates.

azureTenantID.description: The Azure account tenant ID for this provisioner. This ID is the Directory ID available in the Azure Active Directory properties.
azureTenantID.title: Azure Tenant ID

basicAuth.description: Configures provisioner webhook requests to include an Authorization header with these credentials. Optional for `EXTERNAL` webhook servers; not allowed with hosted webhook servers. At most one of `bearerToken` and `basicAuth` may be set.
basicAuth.title: Basic Auth

browser.description: Configuration to use a credential for browser mTLS.

credentials.description: The UUID of credentials that the Smallstep agent will configure to use this browser integration.

id.description: The UUID of this protected browser configuration.

matchAddresses.description: List of URLs the configuration applies to.

name.description: A human-friendly display name for this configuration.

browser.title: Browser Device Connection

browserAccount.description: Configuration to use a client certificate.
browserAccount.title: Browser

certificateField.description: A certificate field that takes a single string value, e.g. Common Name. Static values are used as a fallback when device metadata is not present.

deviceMetadata.description: A value populated from a key in the device's metadata. The special value `smallstep:identity` refers to the device's assigned user. If no value is found in the device's metadata at the specified key then the static value will be used.

certificateField.title: Certificate Field

certificateFieldList.description: A certificate field that accepts multiple string values, e.g. SANs.

deviceMetadata.description: Values populated from keys in the device's metadata. The special value `smallstep:identity` refers to the device's assigned user.

certificateFieldList.title: Certificate Field List

id.description: A UUID identifying this credential. Read only.

credentialCertificate.description: Configuration for the certificate of a managed credential.

authorityID.description: A UUID identifying the authority that issues certificates for the credential.

duration.description: The certificate lifetime. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

credentialCertificate.title: Credential Certificate

credentialFiles.description: Configuration for files that will be written when a managed credential is issued.

crtFile.description: The filepath where the certificate is to be stored.

gid.description: GID of the files where the credential is stored.

keyFile.description: The filepath where the key is to be stored.

keyFormat.description: The format used to encode the private key. For X509 keys the default format is PKCS#8. The classic format is PKCS#1 for RSA keys, SEC 1 for ECDSA keys, and PKCS#8 for ED25519 keys. For SSH keys the default format is always the OPENSSH format. When a hardware module is used to store the keys the default will be a JSON representation of the key, except on Linux tss2 will be used.

mode.description: Permission bits of the files where the credential is stored.

rootFile.description: The filepath where the root certificate is to be stored.

uid.description: UID of the files where the credential is stored.

credentialKey.description: The attributes of the cryptographic key. Key `type` and `protection` are required unless the `pubFile` is set.

protection.description: Whether to use a hardware module to store the private key. If set to `NONE` no hardware module will be used. `HARDWARE_WITH_FALLBACK` can only be used with the key file format `DEFAULT`.

pubFile.description: A CSR or SSH public key to use instead of generating one. Cannot be used in conjunction with key type, key protection, key file or key file format.

type.description: The key type used. The current default type is `ECDSA_P256` but is not fixed at the time the credential resource is created - new keys generated for this credential in the future may have a different type.

credentialKey.title: Credential Key

approvedAt.description: Timestamp in RFC3339 format when the device was approved to connect to company resources. Read only.

connected.description: The device is currently connected to Smallstep. Read only.

enrolledAt.description: Timestamp in RFC3339 format when the device first connected to Smallstep. Read only.

highAssurance.description: The device has been issued certificates using high assurance device attestation. Read only.

hostID.description: The identifier for the smallstep agent on the device.

id.description: A UUID identifying this device. Read only.

lastSeen.description: Timestamp in RFC3339 format when the device last connected to Smallstep. Read only.

device.description: A device represents a computer that can be issued x509 certificates for securely connecting to company resources.

deviceAssurance.description:
The assurance level of the device.
High-assurance devices are those that have a user binding, have been approved, and have performed cryptographic
remote attestation.

deviceDiscoverySource.description: The source from which this device was discovered.

deviceDisplayId.description:
An opaque identifier that may be used to link this device to an external inventory.
This field may be populated with a value derived from data synced from your team's MDMs.
Setting this value explicitly will mask any MDM-derived value.

deviceDisplayName.description:
A friendly name for the device.
This field may be populated with a value derived from data synced from your team's MDMs.
Setting this value explicitly will mask any MDM-derived value.

deviceLifecyclePatch.description:
Updating a device's lifecycle state can be used to control behavior of credential issuance, as well
as inform authorization decisions of integrations, such as RADIUS.

deviceLifecycleStatus.description:
The lifecycle status of a device indicates its ongoing management
state.

deviceMetadata.description: A map of key-value pairs available as template data when a provisioner with a webhook is used to issue a certificate to a device.

deviceOS.description:
The device operating system.
This field may be populated with a value derived from data synced from your team's MDMs.
Setting this value explicitly will mask any MDM-derived value.

deviceOwnership.description:
Whether the device is owned by the user or the company.
This field may be populated with a value derived from data synced from your team's MDMs.
Setting this value explicitly will mask any MDM-derived value.

devicePatch.description:
Data that can be modified on an existing device.

Some device fields may be populated with a value derived from data synced from your team's MDMs.
Any data that is explicitly set by API or with the Smallstep dashboard will mask MDM-derived values.
Use this list to specify fields that should be unset. If the device includes data synced from an MDM
this will result in the fields being populated with values derived from the MDM data. Otherwise the
removed fields will be empty.

deviceRequest.description: Data that can be added to a device record.

permanentIdentifier.description: The cryptographic identity of the device. High-assurance devices are only issued certificates when this identifier is attested by a trusted source. All devices with the same permanent identifier appear as a single device in the Smallstep API. For Windows and Linux devices this is the hash of the TPM endorsement key and for Apple devices it is the serial number.

deviceSerial.description:
The serial number of the device.
This field may be populated with a value derived from data synced from your team's MDMs.
Setting this value explicitly will mask any MDM-derived value.

deviceTags.description: A set of tags that can be used to group devices.

deviceUser.description: The user that a device is assigned to. A device cannot be approved for high-assurance certificates until a user has been assigned to it.

displayName.description: Full name of the user the device is assigned to. Synced from team's identity provider. Read only.

email.description:
Email of the user the device is assigned to.
This field may be populated with a value derived from data synced from your team's MDMs.
Setting this value explicitly will mask any MDM-derived value.

deviceUserPatch.description: The user that a device is assigned to. A device cannot be approved for high-assurance certificates until it has an assigned user.

email.description:
Email of the user the device is assigned to.
This field may be populated with a value derived from data synced from your team's MDMs.
Setting this value explicitly will mask any MDM-derived value.

authorityID.description: A UUID identifying the authority to issue certificates for the account. If not provided, a new authority will be created.

crtFile.description: The filepath where the certificate is to be stored.

duration.description: The certificate lifetime. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

gid.description: GID of the files where the certificate is stored.

keyFile.description: The filepath where the key is to be stored.

mode.description: Permission bits of the files where the certificate is stored.

rootFile.description: The filepath where the root certificate is to be stored.

uid.description: UID of the files where the certificate is stored.

id.description: A UUID identifying this endpoint configuration. Read only.

endpointConfiguration.description: An endpoint configuration represents the details for issuing a certificate a device uses to connect to a protected resource.

endpointConfigurationRequest.description: The configuration settings of an endpoint.

extendedType.description: The extended type of this endpoint, which allows for further configuration to be specified using the `extendedTypeConfiguration` field.

kind.description:
The kind of endpoint.

Account-kind endpoints should be used for human users accessing protected resources. For account-kind endpoints, the `extendedType` and `extendedTypeConfiguration` fields are required.

Device-kind endpoints should be used for devices or machines accessing protected resources.

Workload-kind endpoints should be used issuing credentials to programs running on the device or machine.

name.description: A human-friendly name for this endpoint on devices.

endpointKeyInfo.description: The attributes of the cryptographic key.

format.description: The format used to encode the private key. For X509 keys the default format is PKCS#8. The classic format is PKCS#1 for RSA keys, SEC 1 for ECDSA keys, and PKCS#8 for ED25519 keys. For SSH keys the default format is always the OPENSSH format. When a hardware module is used to store the keys the default will be a JSON representation of the key, except on Linux where tss2 will be used.

protection.description: Whether to use a hardware module to store the private key for a workload certificate. If set to `NONE` no hardware module will be used. If set to `DEFAULT` a hardware module will only be used with format `TSS2`. `HARDWARE_WITH_FALLBACK` can only be used with the key format `DEFAULT`.

pubFile.description: A CSR or SSH public key to use instead of generating one.

type.description: The key type used. The current DEFAULT type is ECDSA_P256.

endpointKeyInfo.title: Key Management Info

endpointReloadInfo.description: The properties used to reload a service.

method.description: Ways an endpoint can reload a certificate. `AUTOMATIC` means the process is able to detect and reload new certificates automatically. `CUSTOM` means a custom command must be run to trigger the workload to reload the certificates. `SIGNAL` will configure the agent to send a signal to the process in `pidFile`. `DBUS` will use the systemd system bus to issue a `try-reload-or-restart` job for unit specified by `unitName`. `PLATFORM` uses a method specific to the operating system.

pidFile.description: File that holds the pid of the process to signal. Required when method is SIGNAL.

signal.description: The signal to send to a process when a certificate should be reloaded. Required when method is SIGNAL.

unitName.description: The systemd unit name to reload when a certificate should be reloaded. Required when method is DBUS.

endpointReloadInfo.title: Managed Endpoint Reload Info

example.message: ID must be a valid UUID

caChain.description: The certificate authority bundle that client certificates must chain up to.

networkAccessServerIP.description: The public IP address of your network equipment that clients connect to.

issuer.description: The URL of this identity provider.

jwksEndpoint.description: The URL where relying parties can read the provider's public key.

tokenEndpoint.description: The URL where relying parties exchange authorization codes for identity tokens.

trustRoots.description: The CA used to verify clients at the authorize endpoint.

lifecycleFailureURI.description: Where user-agents will be sent when the device lifecycle is not active.

redirectURI.description: Where user-agents will be sent after authentication attempts.

secret.description: The secret relying parties will use to authenticate when exchanging an authorization code for an id token.

ikeV2Config.description: Configuration for VPNs that use the IKEv2 protocol.

caChain.description: The certificate authority bundle that client certificates must chain up to.

eap.description: Whether or not EAP is enforced on this VPN server.

remoteID.description: Typically, the common name of the remote server. Defaults to the remote address.

instanceAge.description: The maximum age of an instance that should be allowed to obtain a certificate. Limits certificate issuance to new instances to mitigate the risk of credential-misuse from instances that don't need a certificate. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).
instanceAge.title: Instance Age Cloud Provisioner

clientCA.description: The CA that the RADIUS server will trust to verify clients.

id.description: The UUID of this managed RADIUS server.

name.description: A descriptive name for this resource. Must be unique across the team.

nasIPs.description: The IP addresses the Network Access Server (NAS) may connect from to the RADIUS server.

secret.description:
The credential the Network Access Server (NAS) uses to connect to the RADIUS server.
Only included when the `secret` parameter is set in the query string.

serverCA.description: The CA that supplicants should use to verify the RADIUS server.

serverHostname.description: The hostname of the RADIUS server.

serverIP.description: The IP address of the RADIUS server.

serverPort.description: The port of the RADIUS server.

critical.description: Whether or not name constraints are marked critical.

newAuthority.description: The body of a request to create a new authority.

activeRevocation.description: Whether to enable CRL and OCSP on an advanced authority.

adminEmails.description: Users that will have admin access to manage the authority.

subdomain.description: The new authority will be available at <subdomain>.<team slug>.ca.smallstep.com.

type.description: Create either a devops or advanced authority.

newAuthorityCsr.description: Body of a request to create a new X509 advanced authority with an external root.

activeRevocation.description: Whether to enable CRL and OCSP on the authority.

subdomain.description: The new authority will be available at <subdomain>.<team slug>.ca.smallstep.com.

newAuthorityCsr.title: New Authority CSR

newPlatform.description: Configuration to create a new platform.

platformType.description: Determines which set of fields to use in platformConfiguration. Cannot be changed.

oidcProvisioner.description: A [provisioner](https://smallstep.com/docs/step-ca/provisioners/#oauthoidc-single-sign-on) that is configured to trust and accept an OAuth provider's ID tokens for authentication. By default, the issued certificate will use the subject (sub) claim from the identity token as its subject. The value of the token's email claim is also included as an email SAN in the certificate.

admins.description: The emails of admin users in an OpenID Connect provisioner. These users will not have restrictions in the certificates to sign.

clientID.description: The id used to validate the audience in an OpenID Connect token.

clientSecret.description: The secret used to obtain the OpenID Connect tokens.

domains.description: The domains used to validate the email claim in an OpenID Connect provisioner.

groups.description: The group list used to validate the groups extension in an OpenID Connect token.

listenAddress.description: The callback address used in the OpenID Connect flow.

tenantID.description: The tenant-id used to replace the templatized tenantid value in the OpenID Configuration.

oidcProvisioner.title: OIDC Provisioner

platform.description: A platform with a list of configurations.
platform.title: Platform

policyMatchCriteria.description: Policy to select the devices an account is assigned to. An empty policy indicates an account will be provisioned for all devices.

assurance.description: Assurance levels that devices must match.

operatingSystem.description: Operating systems that devices must match.

ownership.description: Ownership values that devices must match.

source.description: Registration sources that devices must match.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the provisioner was created in RFC 3339 format. Generated server-side.

id.description: A UUID identifying this provisioner. Generated server-side when the provisioner is created.

provisioner.description: [Provisioners](https://smallstep.com/docs/step-ca/provisioners/) are methods of using the CA to get certificates with different modes of authorization.

-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY -----

provisioner.title: Provisioner

provisionerClaims.description: A set of constraints configuring how this provisioner can be used to issue certificates.

allowRenewalAfterExpiry.description: Allow renewals for expired certificates generated by this provisioner.

defaultHostSSHCertDuration.description: The default duration for an SSH host certificate generated by this provisioner. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

defaultTLSCertDuration.description: The default duration for an x509 certificate generated by this provisioner. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

defaultUserSSHCertDuration.description: The default duration for an SSH user certificate generated by this provisioner. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

disableRenewal.description: Disable renewal for all certificates generated by this provisioner.

enableSSHCA.description: Allow this provisioner to be used to generate SSH certificates.

maxHostSSHCertDuration.description: The maximum duration for an SSH host certificate generated by this provisioner. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

maxTLSCertDuration.description: The maximum duration for an x509 certificate generated by this provisioner. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

maxUserSSHCertDuration.description: The maximum duration for an SSH user certificate generated by this provisioner. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

minHostSSHCertDuration.description: The minimum duration for an SSH host certificate generated by this provisioner. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

minTLSCertDuration.description: The minimum duration for an x509 certificate generated by this provisioner. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

minUserSSHCertDuration.description: The minimum duration for an SSH user certificate generated by this provisioner. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

provisionerClaims.title: Provisioner Claims

provisionerOptions.description: Options that apply when issuing certificates with this provisioner.
provisionerOptions.title: Provisioner Options

provisionerWebhook.description: A [webhook](https://smallstep.com/docs/step-ca/webhooks/) to call when a certificate request is being processed.

bearerToken.description: Webhook requests will include an Authorization header with the token. Optional for `EXTERNAL` webhook servers; not allowed with hosted webhook servers. At most one of `bearerToken` and `basicAuth` may be set.

collectionSlug.description: For HOSTED_ATTESTATION webhooks, the collectionSlug is a reference to the collection that holds the devices that may be issued certificates. This collection must already exist. Required for `HOSTED_ATTESTATION` webhook servers; not allowed for `EXTERNAL`.

disableTLSClientAuth.description: The CA will not send a client certificate when requested by the webhook server. Optional for `EXTERNAL` webhook servers; not allowed with hosted webhook servers.

id.description: UUID identifying this webhook. Generated server-side when the webhook is created. Will be sent to the webhook server in every request in the `X-Smallstep-Webhook-ID` header.

kind.description:
The webhook kind indicates how and when it is called.

ENRICHING webhooks are called before rendering the certificate template. They have two functions. First, they must allow the certificate request or it will be aborted. Second, they can return additional data to be referenced in the certificate template. The payload sent to the webhook server varies based on whether an X509 or SSH certificate is to be signed and based on the type of provisioner.

name.description: The name of the webhook. For `ENRICHING` webhooks, the returned data can be referenced in the certificate under the path `.Webhooks.<name>`. Must be unique to the provisioner.

secret.description: The shared secret used to authenticate the payload sent to the webhook server. Generated server-side. This is returned only for `EXTERNAL` webhook servers and only once, at the time of creation.

serverType.description:
An EXTERNAL webhook server is not operated by Smallstep. The caller must use the returned ID and secret to configure the server.

A HOSTED_ATTESTATION webhook server is hosted by Smallstep and must be used with an `ENRICHING` webhook type and an ACME Attestation provisioner. The webhook server will verify the attested permanent identifier exists as the ID of an instance in the configured collection. The data of the instance in the collection will be added to the template data.

url.description: The URL of the webhook server. Required for `EXTERNAL` webhook servers; read-only for hosted webhook servers.

provisionerWebhook.title: Provisioner Webhook

valueFromCertificate.description: The object identifier (OID) of a field in the supplicant certificate. The RADIUS server will set the value of the reply attribute to the value of the field.

scepProvisioner.description: The [SCEP provisioner](https://smallstep.com/docs/step-ca/provisioners/#scep) grants certificates to clients using the SCEP protocol. This type is currently experimental and subject to change.

autogenerateDecrypter.description: Automatically generate a new RSA decrypter instead of providing one.

decrypterCertificate.description: A pem-encoded certificate for an RSA public key.

decrypterKey.description: The pem-encoded RSA private key, corresponding to the certificate public key.

decrypterKeyPassword.description: The password for the pem-encoded RSA private key, if any.

encryptionAlgorithmIdentifier.description:
The encryption algorithm used for encrypting responses to the SCEP client:
 * `DES_CBC`    - [DES in CBC mode](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8018.html#appendix-B.2.1) - `1.3.14.3.2.7`
 * `AES_128_CBC` - [AES-128 in CBC mode](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8018.html#appendix-B.2.5) - `2.16.840.1.101.3.4.1.2`
 * `AES_128_GCM` - [AES-128 in GCM mode](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5084.html#section-3.2) - `2.16.840.1.101.3.4.1.6`
 * `AES_256_CBC` - [AES-256 in CBC mode](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3565.html#section-4.1) - `2.16.840.1.101.3.4.1.42`
 * `AES_256_GCM` - [AES-256 in GCM mode](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5084.html#section-3.2) - `2.16.840.1.101.3.4.1.46`

excludeIntermediate.description: Exclude the intermediate CA certificate in SCEP responses.

forceCN.description: Force one of the SANs to become the Common Name, if a Common Name is not provided.

includeRoot.description: Include the root CA certificate in SCEP responses.

minimumPublicKeyLength.description: The minimum number of bits for RSA public keys accepted in SCEP requests.

scepProvisioner.title: SCEP Provisioner

sshFields.description: Populate certificate fields using static names or device metadata.
sshFields.title: SSH Certificate Fields

sshOptions.description: Options that apply when issuing SSH certificates

template.description: A JSON representation of the SSH certificate to be created. [More info](https://smallstep.com/docs/step-ca/templates/#ssh-templates).

templateData.description: A map of data that can be used by the certificate template.

sshOptions.title: Provisioner SSH Options

credentials.description: The UUID of credentials that the Smallstep agent will configure to use this VPN integration.

id.description: The UUID of this protected VPN configuration.

name.description: A human-friendly display name for this configuration.

vpn.description: Configuration to use a credential to connect a device to a VPN.
vpn.title: VPN Device Connection

vpnAccount.description: Configuration to connect a device to a VPN.

autojoin.description: Whether or not clients should automatically connect to the network.

remoteAddress.description: The public IP address of the VPN server.

vpnAccount.title: VPN

vpnVendor.description: For SSL-type VPN connections, the vendor of the VPN.

wifi.description: Configuration to use a credential to connect to a protected Wi-Fi network.

autojoin.description: Whether or not clients should automatically connect to the network.

credentials.description: The UUID of credentials that the Smallstep agent will configure to use this Wi-Fi integration.

hidden.description: Whether or not the SSID is broadcast by the access points.

id.description: The UUID of this protected Wi-Fi configuration.

name.description: A human-friendly display name for this resource.

radiusServerCA.description: The certificate bundle used to verify the RADIUS server.

radiusServerDomain.description: Require the RADIUS server certificate to contain this DNS name as a SAN or CommonName. Supported on Windows.

ssid.description: The name of the wireless network that clients connect to.

wifi.title: Wi-Fi

wifiAccount.description: Configuration to connect a device to a protected WiFi network.

autojoin.description: Whether or not clients should automatically connect to the network.

caChain.description: The certificate authority bundle that client certificates must chain up to.

externalRadiusServer.description: Whether or not this network is configured to use Smallstep or external RADIUS.

hidden.description: Whether or not the SSID is broadcast by the access points.

networkAccessServerIP.description: The public IP address of your network equipment that clients connect to.

securityProtocol.description: The security protocol that the wireless network is configured for.

ssid.description: The name of the wireless network clients connect to.

wifiAccount.title: WiFi

x509Fields.description: Populate certificate fields using using static names or device metadata.
x509Fields.title: X.509 Certificate Fields

x509Issuer.description: A Customized X509 issuer for an authority.

duration.description: The certificate lifetime. Parsed as a [Golang duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration).

x509Issuer.title: X509 Issuer

x509Options.description: Options that apply when issuing x509 certificates.

template.description: A JSON representation of the x509 certificate to be created. [More info](https://smallstep.com/docs/step-ca/templates/#x509-templates).

templateData.description: A map of data that can be used by the certificate template.

x509Options.title: Provisioner X509 Options

x5cProvisioner.description: A [provisioner](https://smallstep.com/docs/step-ca/provisioners/#x5c---x509-certificate) that authenticates a certificate request with an existing x509 certificate.

roots.description: A list of pem-encoded x509 certificates. Any certificate bundle that chains up to any of these roots can be used in a certificate request.

x5cProvisioner.title: X5C Provisioner

mtls.description: Use a client certificate issued by a trusted root to get short-lived bearer tokens with the step CLI.

info.description:
# Getting Started
First you'll need to get an API token from the Team Settings page of your [Smallstep dashboard](https://smallstep.com/app).

Then you can try [listing certificates](/operations/list-certificates) for your first API call:
```
set +o history
echo "Authorization: Bearer [your API token]" > api_headers
set -o history
curl -H @api_headers https://gateway.smallstep.com/api/certificates
```

More resources:
* [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/smallstep/terraform-provider-smallstep)
* [Python Client](https://github.com/smallstep/smallstep-python)

Returns a CSR for the authority's intermediate issuer that must be signed and submitted to the `/authorities/:id/root` endpoint before the authority will be able to issue certificates.

post.description:
Finalize creation of a new advanced Authority with external root by submitting the signed certificate for the CSR previously returned from the `/authorities/csr` endpoint.
Also submit the root certificate and a name for the root.
The authority will be ready to issue certificates after this operation completes.
